datasets.name: Shifaa_Arabic_Mental_Health_Consultations
datasets.description:
Shifaa Arabic Mental Health Consultations is a high-quality dataset
 designed to advance Arabic medical language models.
This dataset provides 35,648 real-world medical consultations, covering a wide range of mental health concerns.
datasets.notes:
📊 Dataset Summary
Size: 35,648 consultations
Main Specializations: 7
Specific Diagnoses: 123
Languages: Arabic (العربية)
Why This Dataset?
🔹 Lack of Arabic medical data: Most NLP datasets focus on English, leaving a gap in Arabic medical AI.
🔹 Detailed Q&A format: Answers provide in-depth context on symptoms, diagnoses, and treatments.
🔹 Structured Hierarchy: Data is categorized into Main Specialization → Specific Diagnosis.
🔹 High Quality: Data is cleaned, free from missing values & duplicates.

📂 Dataset Features
Each row in the dataset contains:

Column Name	Description
Question Title	Short summary of the consultation
Question	Full question asked by the patient
Answer	Doctor’s detailed response
Doctor Name	Name of the responding doctor
Consultation Number	Unique consultation identifier
Date of Answer	When the answer was provided
Hierarchical Diagnosis	Main Specialization → Specific Diagnosis
🖼️ Data Distribution
Data Distribution

7 Main Specializations (e.g., Depression, Anxiety, PTSD)
123 Specific Diagnoses categorized under specializations
Varied Question Lengths ranging from short inquiries to complex cases.

datasets.name: Mental Health Darija
datasets.description: Mental Health Darija is a multilingual (English and Moroccan Darija) dataset for mental health text classification. Each example contains parallel text: an English sentence (text_en) and its Darija (Moroccan Arabic) translation (text), with a single label indicating the mental health category. The dataset supports classification into seven categories: Anxiety, Bipolar, Depression, Normal, Personality disorder, Stress, and Suicidal. It is intended for research and applications in mental health detection and support in under-resourced languages.
datasets.notes:
MentalHealth-Darija Dataset

Language: Moroccan Darija and English (parallel corpus)

Task: Multi-class mental health text classification.

Size: 51,093 instances.

Labels (7): Anxiety, Bipolar, Depression, Normal, Personality Disorder, Stress, and Suicidal.

Data Format: Each instance contains an English sentence (text_en), its Darija counterpart (text), and a mental health label.

Example:

English: "I've been so restless lately."
Darija: "كنحس براسي مقلق هاد الأيام"
Label: Anxiety

Class Distribution: The dataset is imbalanced, with Normal (16,040) and Depression (15,094) being the largest classes, while Personality Disorder (895) is the smallest.

Dataset Split: No predefined train, validation, or test sets are provided. Researchers are recommended to create their own stratified splits.

Purpose: Developed to support mental health detection and NLP research for Moroccan Darija, a low-resource Arabic dialect.

License: MIT License.

Authors: Abderrahmane Moujar and Otmane El Bourki.

datasets.name: Chezlong
datasets.description: Technology has opened up a new era of mental health care and information gathering, providing new means to access aid, connect to a peer counselor, and track progress. Chezlong is an Arabic text-based chatbot powered by the most modern NLP techniques. It aims to give guidance to those who need quick mental support. The development cycle is based on using the GPT-3.5 Large Language Model for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). RAG is used on Arabic psychological articles, originally scraped from the nafsy.net website. Cleaning, preprocessing, and topic modeling have been applied to prepare and explore the dataset. The chunked dataset is stored as vectors in Pinecone. Similarity search is done to retrieve query-related source knowledge from the dataset to answer a user query.
datasets.notes:
Cleaned and preprocessed Arabic psychological texts scraped from nafsy.net using NLTK and HuggingFace.
Applied BERTopic modeling to explore topic distributions and deduplicate the data.
Synthesized a QA dataset from the original corpus using GPT to support potential fine-tuning.
Stored chunked text embeddings in Pinecone to enable scalable and efficient semantic search.
Implemented similarity search to retrieve contextually relevant passages for user queries.
Prompt-engineered the engine's query to integrate GPT-3.5 with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), ensuring domain-relevant answers.
Connected all previous components within a LangChain pipeline for end-to-end user query handling.
Experimented with multiple LLMs and semantic embedding models to optimize performance for Arabic.

datasets.name: MedQA-Darija-MultiLingual
datasets.description:
MedQA-Darija-MultiLingual
The largest open trilingual medical Q&A dataset with directly-playable speech audio for English, French, and Moroccan Darija.

A research dataset for the BRAIN HEALTH initiative, designed for multilingual medical NLP, low-resource speech recognition, healthcare chatbots, and clinical education tools targeting Morocco and the broader Maghreb region.
datasets.notes:
Medical-QA Trilingual Dataset

Languages: English, French, and Moroccan Darija (Arabic script).

Task: Medical Question Answering (Medical QA), Multilingual NLP, and Speech-based Healthcare Applications.

Size: 267,627 question-answer pairs.

Data Sources:

Real-world data: 163,488 pairs collected from open medical corpora and publicly available medical consultation websites.
Synthetic data: 104,139 pairs generated and validated using multiple Large Language Models (LLMs).

Modalities: Text and speech. Each sample includes six audio files corresponding to the question and answer in the three supported languages.

Medical Coverage: 71 medical specialties, including general medicine, mental health, cardiology, oncology, dermatology, pediatrics, dentistry, radiology, and surgery.

Schema: Each record contains a question-answer pair in English, French, and Moroccan Darija, along with associated audio recordings.

Quality Assurance: The dataset undergoes a three-stage validation pipeline involving outlier detection, causal consistency verification, and linguistic/medical entity validation to ensure data reliability.

Applications: Medical question answering systems, multilingual healthcare chatbots, speech-enabled medical assistants, machine translation, and low-resource language research.

Dataset Size: Approximately 94 GB including more than 427,000 MP3 audio files.

datasets.name: kurtis_mental_health_final
datasets.description: Mental Health Conversational Dataset Language: English. Task: Mental Health Dialogue Generation, Conversational AI, and Empathetic Response Generation. Size: Approximately 4,500 training samples.
datasets.notes:
Mental Health Conversational Dataset (kurtis_mental_health_final)

Language: English.

Task: Mental Health Dialogue Generation, Conversational AI, and Empathetic Response Generation.

Size: 11,491 samples.

Data Format: Each instance contains a mental health-related question (question), a response (answer), and corresponding summaries (summary and answer_summary).

Data Type: Synthetic and curated conversational question-answer pairs designed to simulate realistic mental health discussions.

Applications: Training and fine-tuning mental health chatbots, evaluating conversational models, and developing systems capable of generating empathetic and contextually appropriate responses.

Data Characteristics: The dataset focuses on mental health support scenarios and contains conversations covering emotional well-being, psychological concerns, and supportive interactions.

Limitations: The dataset is intended for research purposes only and should not be used for clinical diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or as a replacement for professional mental health services.

Purpose: Created to provide a foundational resource for developing safe and supportive conversational AI systems in the mental health domain.

datasets.name: mental_health_therapy
datasets.description: This dataset is a combination of a real-therapy conversation from a conselchat forum and a synthetic discussion generated with chatGpt.

datasets.name: counsel-chat
datasets.description: Scrape of Counselchat.com's forum. CounselChat.com is an example of an expert community. It is a platform to help counselors build their reputation and make meaningful contact with potential clients. On the site, therapists respond to questions posed by clients, and users can like responses that they find most helpful. It’s a nice idea and lends itself to some interesting data. This data contains expert responses by licensed clinicialns to questions posed by individuals.
datasets.notes:
CounselChat Expert Counseling Dataset

Language: English.

Task: Mental Health Counseling Response Understanding and Generation.

Size: Scraped dataset (exact split sizes not specified).

Data Format: Each instance contains a unique question identifier (questionID), question title (questionTitle), full user question text (questionText), question link (questionLink), topic category (topic), therapist information (therapistInfo), therapist profile URL (therapistURL), therapist response (answerText), number of upvotes (upvotes), and dataset split (split).

Data Type: Real-world expert-written mental health counseling Q&A data collected from CounselChat, where licensed therapists respond to user-submitted mental health questions.

Applications: Training and evaluating mental health conversational AI systems, counselor-style response generation, empathetic dialogue modeling, and research on expert therapeutic communication.

Data Characteristics: The dataset contains structured counselor–client interactions with topic labels and community feedback (upvotes), reflecting authentic professional mental health responses.

Limitations: The dataset is not anonymized and may contain identifiable therapist information. It should not be used for clinical diagnosis or as a substitute for professional mental health care.

Purpose: Created to address the lack of high-quality counselor-generated mental health datasets and to support NLP research on expert-driven therapeutic dialogue systems.

datasets.name: mental_health_counseling_conversations
datasets.description: This dataset is a compilation of high-quality, real one-on-one mental health counseling conversations between individuals and licensed professionals. Each exchange is structured as a clear question–answer pair, making it directly suitable for fine-tuning or instruction-tuning language models that need to handle sensitive, empathetic, and contextually aware dialogue.
datasets.notes:
Amod Mental Health Counseling Conversations Dataset

Language: English.

Task: Mental Health Counseling Dialogue Generation, Instruction Tuning, and Empathetic Response Modeling.

Size: Large-scale real counseling dataset (exact number not specified in this description; widely downloaded >100,000 times).

Data Format: Each instance contains a user context/question (Context) and a professional counselor response (Response), structured as a direct question–answer pair suitable for instruction tuning.

Data Type: Real-world one-on-one counseling conversations collected from public mental health platforms and written by licensed professionals.

Applications: Training and fine-tuning empathetic chatbots, mental health conversational AI, instruction-following models, and safety-aware dialogue systems capable of handling sensitive psychological content.

Data Characteristics: The dataset contains high-quality therapeutic exchanges emphasizing empathy, advice-giving, and context-aware psychological support, with anonymized user information and professionally written responses.

Limitations: The dataset contains sensitive mental health content and must be used responsibly. It is not suitable for clinical diagnosis or autonomous real-world mental health intervention.

Purpose: Created to support the development of safe, compassionate, and context-aware language models for mental health counseling applications.

datasets.name: MentalChat16K
datasets.description: This dataset consists of 9,775 synthetic conversations between a counselor and a client, covering 33 mental health topics such as  Relationships,  Anxiety,  Depression,  Intimacy, and  Family Conflict. The conversations were generated using the OpenAI GPT-3.5 Turbo model and a customized adaptation of the Airoboros self-generation framework.
datasets.notes:
The Airoboros framework was used to create a new prompt that provided clear instructions for generating patient queries. These queries were then fed back into the GPT-3.5 Turbo model to generate corresponding responses. The proportion of each topic was specified in the prompt to ensure the synthetic conversations authentically mimic the complexity and diversity of human therapist-client interactions.

This dataset aims to equip language models with exposure to a wide spectrum of psychological conditions and therapeutic strategies, enabling them to engage in more realistic and effective counseling conversations. 🧠

Interview Data 6K
This dataset consists of 6338 question-answer pairs from 378 interview transcripts. The transcripts are collected from an ongoing clinical trial transcribed by human experts based on audio recordings of behavioral intervention sessions between behavior health coaches and caregivers of individuals in palliative or hospice care

We employed the local Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 model, which is a state-of-the-art lightweight LLM to paraphrase and summarize interview transcripts documents. We fed each page of transcripts into the model and provided instructions (see Table 1) to summarize the page into a single round of conversation between the caregiver and the behavioral health coach. Subsequently, we filtered out any conversations with less than 40 words in the question and answer.

📊 Dataset Characteristics
Number of Conversations: 9,775 
Topics Covered:  Relationships,  Anxiety,  Depression,  Intimacy,  Family Conflict, and 28 other mental health topics
Language: English 🇺🇸
Generation Method: OpenAI GPT-3.5 Turbo model with a customized Airoboros self-generation framework
🤖 Dataset Usage
This dataset can be used to train and evaluate language models for counseling and mental health applications, such as chatbots, virtual assistants, and dialogue systems. It provides a diverse and realistic set of conversational scenarios that can help improve the models' understanding of psychological conditions and therapeutic strategies.

🌍 Dataset Limitations
The dataset is entirely synthetic and may not fully capture the nuances and complexities of real-world counseling conversations. Additionally, the dataset is limited to English language conversations and may not be representative of diverse cultural and linguistic contexts.

datasets.name: arnaiztech/llms-mental-health-crisis-responses
datasets.description: This dataset repo contains the response-side artifacts prepared for Hugging Face from the paper Between Help and Harm: An Evaluation of Mental Health Crisis Handling by LLMs, published in JMIR Mental Health.
datasets.notes:
arnaiztech/llms-mental-health-crisis-responses Dataset

Language: English.

Task: Mental Health Crisis Response Evaluation, LLM Response Assessment, and Appropriateness Scoring.

Size: 194,856 samples.

Data Format: The dataset contains model-generated responses (responses/) aligned with example IDs, along with multiple evaluation layers including human raw scores (human_raw), LLM-based judgments (llm_raw), merged evaluation statistics (llm_merged), and sampled review subsets used for agreement analysis (sampled_review_sets).

Data Type: Real-world LLM-generated crisis response dataset combined with human and automated evaluation annotations for mental health crisis handling scenarios.

Applications: Evaluating LLM safety in mental health crisis situations, benchmarking response appropriateness, studying human vs model agreement, comparing evaluator models, and reproducing research findings on crisis-response quality.

Data Characteristics: The dataset includes structured response–evaluation pairs with metadata, provenance tracking, and multiple scoring dimensions (including score, variance, and evaluator agreement metrics).

Limitations: The dataset is not intended for clinical use or therapeutic guidance. It should not be used as a replacement for professional mental health intervention or real-world crisis support systems.

Purpose: Created to support research on the safety, appropriateness, and reliability of large language models in handling mental health crisis conversations and to analyze alignment between human and model evaluations.

datasets.name: arafatanam/Student-Mental-Health-Counseling-10K
datasets.description:
This dataset is derived from the original chillies/student-mental-health-counseling-vn dataset, which contains student mental health counseling conversations in Vietnamese.

In this version, 10,000 randomly sampled rows from the original dataset have been translated into English using Google Translator, making the data more accessible for English-speaking researchers and developers.
datasets.notes:
arafatanam/Student-Mental-Health-Counseling-10K Dataset

Language: English (translated from Vietnamese).

Task: Mental Health Counseling Dialogue Understanding, Text Classification, and Conversational AI.

Size: 10,000 samples.

Data Format: Each instance contains student mental health counseling text originally in Vietnamese, translated into English using GoogleTranslator (deep_translator), stored in CSV format and auto-converted to Parquet, with raw counseling dialogue-style entries.

Data Type: Translated real-world student counseling dataset derived from Vietnamese mental health support conversations.

Applications: Training mental health chatbots, sentiment analysis in counseling contexts, NLP research on student mental health communication, and development of multilingual mental health support systems.

Data Characteristics: The dataset contains student counseling conversations reflecting emotional distress, academic pressure, and psychological well-being, translated into English while preserving original intent.

Limitations: Translation noise may affect semantic accuracy. 
Purpose: Created to provide a translated resource for mental health NLP research, enabling development of AI systems for student counseling and emotional support applications.

datasets.name: AraDepSu
datasets.description: This dataset comprises 15,000 Arabic-language tweets manually annotated for depression-related content. Each tweet is labeled as either "Depression" or "Non-depression," with additional binary flags indicating the presence of negation and intensifiers two key linguistic features often associated with emotional expression.
datasets.notes:
Arabic Mental Health Tweets Dataset

Language: Arabic (Egyptian dialect + MSA + mixed dialects).

Task: Mental Health Text Classification (Depression and Suicidal Ideation Detection).

Size: 20,213 tweets (15,159 training, 5,054 testing).

Data Format: Each instance contains a tweet text (tweet), a mental health label (depressed, suicidal, or neutral/non-depression), and additional linguistic features such as negation_flag and intensifier_flag.

Data Type: Real-world social media posts collected from Twitter and manually annotated for mental health classification.

Applications: Training and evaluating NLP models for depression and suicide risk detection, social media mental health monitoring, and fine-tuning transformer-based models such as MARBERT and AraBERT.

Data Characteristics: The dataset includes Arabic tweets reflecting emotional states, with a mix of dialects and informal language typical of social media communication.

Limitations: Labels are based on manual annotation and may contain subjectivity.
Purpose: Created to support research on automated detection of depression and suicidal ideation from Arabic social media text.

datasets.name: Baghdadi et al. Arabic Suicide Dataset
datasets.description: it was made for An Optimized Deep Learning Approach for Suicide Detection through Arabic Tweets (Research Paper Script)
datasets.notes:
Arabic Suicide Detection Twitter Dataset

Language: Arabic.

Task: Mental Health Text Classification (Suicide Risk Detection).

Size: 2,030 annotated tweets (14,576 raw tweets collected).

Data Format: Each instance contains an Arabic tweet text, manually annotated label (Suicide or Normal), and was processed through cleaning and linguistic preprocessing including normalization, stemming/lemmatization, and stop-word removal.

Data Type: Real-world Twitter posts collected using keyword-based scraping and manually annotated using majority voting.

Applications: Training and evaluating NLP models for suicide detection, mental health monitoring on social media, and fine-tuning Arabic transformer models for safety-sensitive classification tasks.

Data Characteristics: The dataset contains context-rich Arabic tweets expressing or discussing suicidal ideation, with careful annotation to reduce noise such as sarcasm and irrelevant keyword matches.

Limitations: The dataset is small after annotation filtering and may contain annotation subjectivity despite majority voting. It should not be used for clinical diagnosis or real-world intervention without expert supervision.

Purpose: Created to support research on automatic detection of suicidal ideation in Arabic social media content.

datasets.name: AraStress
datasets.description: Collected using CrowdTangle from public Arabic Facebook pages focusing on mental health, wellness, and emotional support
datasets.notes:
Arabic Psychological Stress Facebook Posts Dataset

Language: Arabic.

Task: Psychological Stress Detection and Mental Health Text Classification.

Size: 175,862 Facebook posts.

Data Format: Each instance contains Arabic social media text (Facebook posts) labeled for stress-related content based on targeted psychological stress keywords, along with rich metadata fields including engagement metrics, reaction counts, timestamps, and geographic indicators.

Data Type: Real-world Facebook posts collected from Arabic-speaking users and annotated using keyword-based stress detection criteria, combined with structured metadata for behavioral and engagement analysis.

Applications: Stress detection in Arabic social media, digital well-being analysis, mental health monitoring, sociolinguistic studies of psychological stress expression, and development of NLP models for culturally grounded mental health detection.

Data Characteristics: The dataset captures expressions of psychological stress across Modern Standard Arabic and various regional dialects, enriched with 52 metadata features and contextual engagement signals.

Limitations: Labels are derived from keyword-based heuristics and may include noise or indirect expressions of stress. The dataset should not be used for clinical diagnosis or individual-level psychological assessment.

Purpose: Created to support research on stress detection in Arabic social media and to advance culturally aware mental health NLP systems.

datasets.name: Arabic Trauma Corpus
datasets.description: Scraped from public Facebook pages using trauma-related Arabic keywords (e.g., psychological trauma, traumatic stress)Data is real.
datasets.notes:
Arabic Trauma Facebook Posts Dataset

Language: Arabic.

Task: Trauma-Related Mental Health Text Classification and Discourse Analysis.

Size: 16,529 posts.

Data Format: Each instance contains Arabic Facebook post text related to trauma expressions such as grief, loss, and psychological distress, used for comparative analysis with stress-related discourse datasets.

Data Type: Real-world social media posts collected from Facebook, focusing on trauma-related psychological expressions in Arabic.

Applications: Trauma detection in Arabic social media, comparative studies with stress and other mental health conditions, PTSD-related NLP research, and development of culturally aware mental health classification systems.

Data Characteristics: The dataset captures natural language expressions of trauma, grief, and emotional distress in Arabic social media contexts.

Limitations: Limited metadata availability and lack of detailed annotation schema. The dataset should not be used for clinical diagnosis or direct psychological assessment.

Purpose: Created to support research on trauma-related language patterns in Arabic social media and to enhance mental health NLP understanding of grief and psychological distress expressions.

datasets.name: Arabic Depression Tweets Dataset
datasets.description: This dataset contains 15,000 Arabic tweets annotated for depression detection and includes linguistic feature augmentations to support research in natural language processing (NLP), sentiment analysis, and mental health detection
datasets.notes:
Arabic Depression Detection Twitter Dataset

Language: Arabic.

Task: Mental Health Text Classification (Depression Detection in Social Media).

Size: Public Twitter dataset (exact size not specified in this description).

Data Format: Each instance contains an Arabic tweet (tweet), a binary depression label (label: 1 = Depression, 0 = Non-depression), and linguistic feature flags including negation_flag and intensifier_flag, along with a redundant textual class label (Class).

Data Type: Real-world Arabic Twitter posts collected from public social media sources and manually annotated by native speakers with psychology and linguistics background.

Applications: Training and evaluating depression detection models, linguistic analysis of depressive expression in Arabic, cross-lingual mental health research, and development of clinical decision-support NLP systems.

Data Characteristics: The dataset includes Arabic tweets written in Modern Standard Arabic and various dialects, enriched with linguistically motivated features such as negation and intensifiers to support deeper NLP modeling.

Limitations: The dataset is limited to publicly available tweets and may contain annotation subjectivity. 
Purpose: Created to support research on automatic detection of depression in Arabic social media and to advance computational mental health analysis using machine learning and deep learning approaches.

datasets.name: Reddit C-SSRS Suicide Dataset
datasets.notes:
Reddit C-SSRS Suicide Risk Dataset

Language: English.

Task: Suicide Risk Detection and Severity Classification (C-SSRS-based).

Size: 500 annotated instances (subset of 2,181 Reddit users overall corpus).

Data Format: Each instance contains Reddit user text data annotated for suicide risk severity based on the Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS), with labels representing different levels of suicidal ideation, behavior, and intent.

Data Type: Real-world Reddit posts collected from users discussing or implying mental health conditions, particularly depression and suicidal ideation, with expert psychiatric annotation.

Applications: Suicide risk severity classification, mental health crisis detection, clinical NLP research, development of safety-aware AI systems, and evaluation of risk stratification models.

Data Characteristics: The dataset includes multi-level suicide risk labels (supportive, low, moderate, high, and severe risk categories) annotated by psychiatrists, with domain-specific lexicons and medical knowledge integration.

Limitations: The dataset is small and highly specialized, and annotations reflect expert judgment which may still contain subjectivity. It should not be used for clinical diagnosis or real-world intervention without professional oversight.

Purpose: Created to support research on fine-grained suicide risk detection using social media data and to improve computational models for mental health severity assessment using clinical frameworks like C-SSRS.

datasets.name: DAIC-WOZ Database
datasets.description:
DAIC-WOZ Database
This database contains clinical interviews designed to support the diagnosis of psychological distress conditions such as anxiety, depression, and post-traumatic stress disorder.

These interviews were collected as part of a larger effort to create a computer agent that interviews people and identifies verbal and nonverbal indicators of mental illness (DeVault et al., 2014).  

Data collected include audio and video recordings and extensive questionnaire responses; this part of the corpus includes data from the Wizard-of-Oz interviews, conducted by an animated virtual interviewer called Ellie, controlled by a human interviewer in another room.

datasets.name: MentalQA: An Annotated Arabic Corpus for Questions and Answers of Mental Healthcare
datasets.description:
📊 Dataset Split & Release for Shared Task: Comprehensive Arabic Health Question Answering Shared Task (AraHealthQA 2025)

The full dataset consists of 500 annotated samples. For the purpose of this shared task, we are releasing only the training and development (train+dev) portion:

Train+Dev set: 350 samples

Test set: 150 samples (Released)
datasets.notes:
MentalQA Dataset

Language: English.

Task: Medical and Mental Health Question Answering, Question Classification, and Response Strategy Prediction.

Size: 500 samples.

Data Format: Each instance contains a question (question), an answer (answer), a question type label (final_QT), and an answer strategy label (final_AS).

Data Type: Expert-annotated medical question–answer dataset covering clinical, health, and mental health-related queries with structured taxonomy labels for both questions and responses.

Applications: Medical NLP question answering, clinical reasoning classification, answer strategy modeling, healthcare dialogue systems, and evaluation of response styles such as informational, directive, and emotional support.

Data Characteristics: The dataset includes categorized medical questions spanning diagnosis, treatment, anatomy and physiology, epidemiology, lifestyle, provider selection, and other domains, paired with structured answer strategy labels (information, direct guidance, emotional support).

Limitations: The dataset is limited in size and domain coverage and should not be used for real clinical decision-making or medical diagnosis without expert supervision.

Purpose: Created to support research on structured medical question answering and to analyze how different types of medical questions are answered using distinct response strategies in healthcare NLP systems.

datasets.name: Mental Health Social Posts Dataset
datasets.description: This dataset contains 1,000 anonymized social media posts labeled for signs of mental health conditions including depression, anxiety, suicidal thoughts, or no apparent issues. It is designed to help researchers and developers build early warning systems, mental health support chatbots, or other AI tools to detect and assist individuals experiencing mental health challenges. The posts are synthetic but modeled to reflect realistic language and emotional cues commonly found on social media.
